pages.text: Biomancy is also a building set — an entire palette of flesh, bone and chitin to build in. Most of it is grown in the $(l:3_machines/bio_forge)Bio-Forge$(); the worked variants are then cut and crafted as ordinary blocks.
pages.title: The Flesh Palette

pages.text: $(bold)The families:$()$(br)$(li)$(bold)Flesh$() — the base block, with packed, fibrous and tubular variants, plus a pillar and a chiselled and an ornamental form.$(li)$(bold)Primal Flesh$() — a tougher grade: smooth, porous, and the worked stairs/slabs/walls of each.$(li)$(bold)Malignant Flesh$() — diseased-looking block, with veins that creep across surfaces.

$(bold)Worked like stone.$() Each family has the full set of slabs, stairs and walls, and the worked forms cut at a stonecutter just as their mineral cousins do. $(flesh)Flesh doors$(), an $(flesh)iris-door$(), fences, a gate, a ladder and spikes round the palette out — enough to build a base that is unmistakably $(italic)alive$().
pages.title: A Living Build

pages.text: $(bold)Light from flesh.$() $(flesh)Bio-Lanterns$() (blue and yellow), the $(flesh)Bloom-Lantern$() and $(flesh)Bloomlight$() are biomancy's light blocks, grown from Bioluminescent Goo. The $(flesh)Tendon Chain$() hangs them.
pages.title: Lights
